prerequisites.value: Knowledge (planes) 3 ranks, must swear allegiance to a single Hell Knight order.

description.value: **Benefit:** Upon taking this feat, you gain proficiency in all of your Hell Knight order âs favored weapons. Additionally, by meditating and inflicting strenuous reckonings upon yourself, you unlock the power behind your orderâs doctrine. Each Hell Knight order has its own unique reckoningâa trial of obedience and pain (described in Hell Knight order ). When coupled with the necessary reflection and meditation, this reckoning takes 1 hour per day to perform. Once you have performed your orderâs reckoning, you gain the benefit of a special ability as indicated in the Reckoning entry for the Hell Knight order to which youâve sworn allegiance. If you have at least 12 Hit Dice, you also gain the first boon granted by the Hell Knight order. If you have at least 16 Hit Dice, you also gain the Hell Knight order âs second boon. If you have 20 Hit Dice or more, you also gain the Hell Knight order âs third boon. Unless a specific duration or number of uses per day is listed in a boonâs description, the boonâs effects are constant. If you ever fail to perform a daily reckoning, you lose all access to the benefits and boons granted by this feat until you next perform the reckoning. You can take this feat only if you are associated with a Hell Knight order, whether youâre in training to become a Hell Knight, already a member, or otherwise aligned with the orderâthe specifics are left to you and the GM to determine. Once per year, you can change your Hell Knight allegiance, but doing so requires the approval of the Hell Knight order you wish to join. If you are ever cast out of the Hell Knight order to which youâve sworn allegiance, you lose the benefits of this feat until you restore yourself in the Hell Knightsâ esteem. The details of this are left to the GMâs discretion.
